text:
TASKER API ENDPOINTS (Tier 1 Kotlin API, port 5563)

Four endpoints for Tasker automation. All use Intent broadcasts to net.dinglisch.android.taskerm.
You must know the exact task/profile/variable names configured in Tasker.

1. POST /tasker/run
   Run a named Tasker task.
   Body: {"task_name": "MyTaskName"}
   Example: curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5563/tasker/run -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"task_name": "MyTaskName"}'

2. GET /tasker/tasks
   Note: Tasker does not expose its task list via API. Returns empty array.
   You must ask the user for task names or check Tasker directly.

3. POST /tasker/variable
   Set a Tasker global variable.
   Body: {"name": "VarName", "value": "some_value"}
   Example: curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5563/tasker/variable -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"name": "MyVar", "value": "hello"}'

4. POST /tasker/profile
   Enable or disable a Tasker profile.
   Body: {"profile_name": "ProfileName", "enabled": true}
   Example: curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5563/tasker/profile -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"profile_name": "MyProfile", "enabled": true}'

IMPORTANT: Tasker must be installed (net.dinglisch.android.taskerm) and the task/profile/variable must already exist in Tasker. These endpoints fire-and-forget via broadcast -- there is no return value from Tasker confirming execution.
